goal: Fix Hub v1 review findings: files index, in-flight work, responsive tables

summary.title: Hub v1 review fixes: files index, open work, responsive tables
summary.what: Addressed the three Hub v1 dogfood review findings. Added a top-level Files index page and sidebar link so generated file-history pages are discoverable. Added an Open work page plus an open_intents field in the HubModel JSON, populated from local non-terminal drafts while skipping stale drafts whose authoritative view status is terminal. Added an open-work notice on the Recent page and made Hub tables horizontally scroll with a mobile-friendly top navigation layout.
summary.why: Hub v1 is meant to validate whether a local static reader helps humans answer intent-history questions faster than CLI log/show/trace. The original version generated useful file pages but hid them, omitted local in-flight work from the team-entry view, and made wide tables hard to read on narrow browser windows. These fixes keep Hub local/read-only while making the main dogfood workflows reachable and usable.
summary.user_goal: Fix the three review findings on a new branch: discoverable file pages, in-flight work visibility, and narrow viewport readability.

decisions.chose: Add a top-level files.html page and sidebar Files link, reusing the existing FileIndex and per-file pages.
decisions.rationale: This resolves the core file-history workflow without adding search, JS, or a new data source. It preserves the original int_41382daf direction that render.go/templates are throwaway while HubModel remains the durable read contract.

decisions.chose: Derive open_intents from local draft files in drafting/sealed_local/proposed states and cross-check them against the mainline view to skip terminal stale drafts.
decisions.rationale: The local Hub should show work a user can resume or review, but should not re-surface stale draft files for intents already merged, abandoned, superseded, or reverted in the authoritative view.

decisions.chose: Expose open work through a separate open.html page and a compact notice on Recent.
decisions.rationale: This keeps Recent focused on sealed intent history while making in-flight local work visible from the entry page.

decisions.chose: Wrap intent tables in a horizontal scroll container and add a narrow-screen layout for the top navigation/sidebar.
decisions.rationale: Hub v1 keeps plain HTML/CSS, but wide read tables should remain usable in the in-app browser and smaller viewports.

rejected.alternative: Add client-side search/filtering for files and intents now.
rejected.reason: Useful later, but the review finding only requires discoverability. A static Files index fixes the immediate dogfood gap without adding JavaScript or extra UI complexity.

rejected.alternative: Import engine.Service or reuse Status() directly for open work.
rejected.reason: The original Hub boundary intentionally depends only on storage.Store to avoid engine import cycles. Reimplementing the small draft rollup locally keeps that boundary intact.

rejected.alternative: Include all draft files unconditionally as open work.
rejected.reason: Draft files can lag after merge/pin. Cross-checking terminal view status avoids stale suggestions that would reduce trust in Hub.

risks: open_intents extends the HubModel JSON contract. It is additive and local-only, but downstream readers that assumed an exact object shape may need to tolerate the new optional field.
Open work is limited to the local drafts cache. It does not show other actors local-only drafting work unless that work has been published into the shared view.
Responsive handling uses horizontal scroll rather than a fully redesigned compact table, so very narrow screens still require lateral scrolling for all columns.
followups: Dogfood whether the Files page is enough or whether a no-JS grouped/searchable file index becomes necessary once file counts grow.
Consider surfacing proposed/shared in-flight work separately from local drafts if teams want Hub to become a broader daily entry point.

architectural_claims: Hub remains local, read-only, static, and derived from storage.Store without importing engine.Service.
HubModel JSON is extended additively with open_intents for local in-flight draft summaries.
Open work treats the mainline view as authoritative for terminal statuses so stale local drafts do not reappear after merge/abandon/supersede/revert.
behavioral_changes: mainline hub export now writes files.html and open.html in addition to the existing pages.
The Hub sidebar now includes Open and Files links.
The Recent page shows a notice when local open intents exist.
The export JSON result now includes open_count.
Hub tables scroll horizontally on narrow viewports and the sidebar collapses into top navigation on small screens.

api_changes.signature: HubModel JSON adds optional open_intents; ExportResult JSON adds open_count

data_model_changes.name: HubOpenIntent / open_intents
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Additive optional JSON field derived from local drafts.

security_implications: No new network IO, server, JavaScript, or write location was introduced.
Open work reads local draft metadata already owned by Mainline and renders it through html/template auto-escaping.
Static export still writes only under the caller-selected output directory.
